skill.name: system-prompt
skill.description: Edit or improve the AI system prompt used in DBX Studio's AI chat. Invoke when the user wants to change how the AI responds, its tone, tool usage order, or response format.
skill.summary: Edit or improve the AI system prompt used in DBX Studio's AI chat. Invoke when the user wants to cha...
skill.author: Dbxstudio

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 24 patterns but manual review confirms all are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is pure documentation (SKILL.md) with no executable code. Backticks are markdown code formatting, path traversal (../) is standard relative path notation, and SAM is part of a variable name, not Windows SAM database. No security risks identified.

high_findings.title: External Commands False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected backticks as shell command execution. Actual content: markdown inline code formatting (e.g., `SYSTEM_PROMPT_WITH_TOOLS`) and code fences (```). No shell commands present.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks in markdown are syntax for inline code, not shell execution. No command execution found.

high_findings.title: Filesystem Path Traversal False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged '../' as path traversal vulnerability. Actual content: standard relative path notation in documentation links.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Relative paths (../../../) are standard documentation conventions, not security vulnerabilities.

content.value_statement: This skill helps users customize how the AI assistant responds in DBX Studio, controlling tone, tool usage order, and response format for better data querying.
actual_capabilities: Locate and edit the streaming system prompt in ai-stream.ts
Update the oRPC provider prompt in providersWithTools.ts
Add or remove AI tools from the Tools Available section
Modify response rules and formatting guidelines
Keep prompt structure consistent across both prompt locations
limitations: Only edits prompts for DBX Studio, not other projects
Cannot add new AI tools, only modify prompt text for existing tools
Does not execute or test the modified prompts
Schema context injection points must be preserved

use_cases.title: Customize AI Response Tone
use_cases.description: Change how the AI assistant speaks to be more friendly, technical, or concise
use_cases.target_user: Team leads who want to customize AI behavior for their team

use_cases.title: Add New Tools to Prompt
use_cases.description: Update the Tools Available section when new tools are added to the codebase

use_cases.title: Improve Query Results
use_cases.description: Modify response rules to show more or less detail in query results
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts who want tailored output formats

prompt_templates.title: Basic Prompt Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the system prompt in DBX Studio to change the response tone to be more friendly.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to customize AI personality

prompt_templates.title: Add New Tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the Tools Available section to include a new tool called 'export_csv' that exports query results to CSV format.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer added a new tool and needs to document it

prompt_templates.title: Change Output Format
prompt_templates.prompt: Modify the response rules to show SQL queries in a code block with syntax highlighting comments.

prompt_templates.title: Sync Both Prompts
prompt_templates.prompt: Make sure both the streaming prompt and oRPC prompt have consistent response rules about showing results first.
prompt_templates.scenario: Ensuring uniform AI behavior across different code paths

output_examples.input: Edit the system prompt to make the AI more concise
output_examples.output: The AI will now prioritize brief, direct answers with minimal explanation before diving into details.

output_examples.input: Add a rule about using charts for numerical data
output_examples.output: Updated Chart Selection Guide section to recommend charts for numerical trends and distributions.

best_practices: Keep both prompts (streaming and oRPC) consistent in style and rules
Always maintain the Tools Available section in sync with actual tools in tools.ts
Preserve the schema injection points (${enhancedPrompt} and Schema:) for context
anti_patterns: Removing the schema injection points which break query context
Making prompts too long that they exceed token limits
Inconsistent rules between streaming and oRPC prompts

faq.question: Where are the system prompts located?
faq.answer: There are two prompts: apps/api/src/routes/ai-stream.ts (streaming) and apps/api/src/orpc/routers/ai/providersWithTools.ts (oRPC).

faq.question: Can I add completely new AI tools?
faq.answer: No, this skill edits prompt text only. To add new tools, you must implement them in tools.ts first.

faq.question: What happens if I break the prompt syntax?
faq.answer: The AI may behave unexpectedly. Always test prompts after editing. Keep the template structure intact.

faq.question: Should both prompts be kept in sync?
faq.answer: Yes, maintaining consistent rules across both prompts ensures uniform AI behavior.

faq.question: Can I change the tool order?
faq.answer: Yes, the Tools Available section is ordered. Change the order to influence when the AI uses each tool.

faq.question: What is the Schema injection point?
faq.answer: ${enhancedPrompt} and Schema: inject live database schema context so the AI knows available tables.
